description: To perceive with the eyes; to pay visual attention. Can also be used as an evidential verb to express direct sensory evidence ('I see that...').
rationale: The 'sh' softens vision; 'i' sharpens focus; 'pe' extends perspective. Two syllables for clear seeing.

description: To perceive with the ears; to attend to sounds. Can also be used as an evidential verb to express reported/hearsay evidence ('I hear that...').
rationale: The 'wh' resonates with sound; 'i' tunes attention; 'pi' receives message. Two syllables for listening.

description: To register an internal, subjective state; to undergo an experience.
rationale: The word 'hisu' combines the breathy, personal sound of 'h' with the soft sensory 's'. The vowels 'i' and 'u' phonetically move from a high, sharp point of perception to a deeper, more resonant internal state, mirroring the act of sensing something and having it become part of one's inner experience. It aligns with the Buddhist-inspired pillar of mindfulness.

concept: To Touch / To Feel with the Skin
description: To perceive something through physical contact; to feel the texture or presence of something with the skin.
rationale: A compositional verb combining 'hisu' (to feel) with the connective syllable '-le'. This composition extends the internal concept of 'feeling' outward to the world, creating the meaning 'to feel outwardly, with connection.'

concept: To Smell / To Perceive with the Breath
description: To perceive an odor or scent by means of the olfactory sense.
rationale: 'whi' is from 'whio' (air/breath), grounding the sense in the act of breathing. The nasal consonant 'n' is directly related to the nose and the act of inhaling a scent.

concept: To Taste / To Perceive with the Mouth
description: To perceive the flavor of something by taking it into the mouth.
rationale: A compound of 'mu' (in/inside) and 'la' (causal 'because of'). It can be poetically understood as 'the reason from within,' capturing the idea that the essential quality of a food is discovered when it is taken inside.
